page.content: The analysis presents two tables detailing diagnostic parameters and gene ontology (GO) terms. In Table 1, the parameters include Sensitivity at 84.0% with a 95% confidence interval (CI) of 79.5–88.5, Specificity at 61.0% with a 95% CI of 55.0–67.1, Positive Predictive Value at 35.0% with a 95% CI of 29.1–40.9, Negative Predictive Value at 93.8% with a 95% CI of 91.1–96.9, and Accuracy at 65.6% with a 95% CI of 60.1–71.9. Table 2 lists various GO terms alongside their respective P-values, genes, and involved miRNAs.

cells.cell_text: positive regulation of programmed cell death

page.content: The term actin cytoskeleton organization has a P-value of 0.023554309, associated with genes RND3, CALD1, LIMCH1, MYH11, and miRNAs miR-802, miR-1322. Similarly, actin filament-based process has a P-value of 0.027802287 with the same genes and miRNAs. The positive regulation of apoptosis is represented by a P-value of 0.029075368, involving genes SERINC3, CDKN2A, ALDH1A3, IGFBP3, CASP2, and miRNAs miR-802, miR-4319, miR-933, miR-1322, miR-3176, miR-1469. The positive regulation of programmed cell death shows a P-value of 0.029725847 with identical gene and miRNA involvement, while the positive regulation of cell death has a P-value of 0.030164333, maintaining the same list of genes and miRNAs.
